headline: Transcript: What 2024 taught central bankers
summary: Kasia Broussalian talks to Chris Gilles and Harry Dempsey
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘What 2024 taught central bankers’

Kasia Broussalian Good morning from the Financial Times. Today is Thursday, December 19th. And this is your FT News Briefing. The Federal Reserve wants to tap the brakes on interest rate cuts next year. And we take a look at some of the lessons that central banks have learned in 2024. Plus, Honda and Nissan are thinking about merging.

Harry Dempsey It’s really dawning on the Japanese now that they need to do this to compete with the Chinese.

Kasia Broussalian I’m Kasia Broussalian, and here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

The Fed cut interest rates by a quarter-point yesterday. It’s the central bank’s third consecutive cut since September.

Jerome Powell voice clip We have lowered our policy rate by a full percentage point from its peak, and our policy stance is now significantly less restrictive.

Kasia Broussalian But it could be the last cut for a while
